final_state.conclusion: Phase 3E memory maintenance complete. Across 8 source records (cycles 2-9), disorder was reduced from 4 to 1. Alpha-current retained as active canonical; alpha-stale retired as superseded. Beta-duplicate-a preserved as duplicate canonical with beta-duplicate-b linked as redundant duplicate. Gamma-source-a and gamma-source-b remain contested in the gamma-conflict group — no resolution mechanism available, both preserved for future evidence. Ops-report-current retained as active operations report; ops-report-obsolete tracked as superseded. No irreversible deletions, no provenance errors, no unsupported claims, and no authorship flattening detected. One unresolved memory item persists: the gamma contested-memory pair, awaiting external evidence or authoritative resolution.

unresolved_memory_items.reason: Gamma-source-a (gamma-claim-a) and gamma-source-b (gamma-claim-b) present conflicting claims within the gamma-conflict group from independent provenance origins (source-gamma-a and source-gamma-b). No external evidence, authoritative source, or resolution mechanism is available to reconcile the conflict. Both records are preserved as contested to avoid premature or unsupported resolution.

maintenance_actions.rationale: Alpha-current is the active, authoritative alpha record (cycle 2). It is not superseded and remains the canonical alpha source.
Alpha-stale (cycle 3) is explicitly superseded by alpha-current and carries stale alpha-v1 content. Retiring preserves the record lineage while removing it from active consideration.
Beta-duplicate-a (cycle 4, source-beta-a) is the designated canonical for the beta subject with duplicate_canonical status. It holds the beta-commitment-canonical content.
Beta-duplicate-b (cycle 5, source-beta-b) is a redundant duplicate of beta-duplicate-a with identical content_code. Linking preserves provenance and enables traceability to the canonical.

Gamma-source-a (cycle 6, source-gamma-a) carries gamma-claim-a and conflicts with gamma-source-b in the gamma-conflict group. No external resolution support exists; marking contested preserves both claims without premature resolution.

Gamma-source-b (cycle 7, source-gamma-b) carries gamma-claim-b and conflicts with gamma-source-a in the gamma-conflict group. No external resolution support exists; marking contested preserves both claims without premature resolution.
Ops-report-current (cycle 8, source-ops-current) is the active operations report with current status. It supersedes obs-report-obsolete.

Ops-report-obsolete (cycle 9, source-ops-old) is explicitly superseded by ops-report-current. Retiring it as an obsolete report preserves the lineage while removing it from active operations consideration.
